The result is the local time of germination. Note the
Moon's place at that time; and this is the longitude of the

86 Rectification of Birth Time
Moon at the time of germination.
v1. Suppose the position of the Moon at germination is not
near the longitude of the lagna or the seventh in the
birth chart, proceed as follows:
Take the Sidereal Time at noon of the germination date. If
the germination appears to be before noon, take the S.T. at
noon of the previous day. Deduct this from the S.T. corre
sponding to the Moon's longitude plus 180 degree rising at
birth. This gives the local time of germination. Then find the
longitude of the Moon for this time.
The date on which the Moon's place corresponds closely to
the degree of the lagna or to that of the seventh house on the
day of birth has to be taken as final. If the Moon is above the
horizon the "A" days have to be added to 273 day; otherwise
they have to be deducted.
